questions.question_text: Evaluate : (i) $\log_3 27$ (ii) $\log_7 \left(\frac{1}{343}\right)$ (iii) $\log_{100} (0.01)$ (iv) $\log_8 128$
Evaluate: (i) $\log_7 1$ (ii) $\log_{34} 34$ (iii) $36^{\log_6 4}$
questions.solution:
(i) We know that $\log_a 1 = 0$, so $\log_7 1 = 0$.
(ii) We know that $\log_a a = 1$, so $\log_{34} 34 = 1$.
(iii) We know that $a^{\log_a x} = x$.
Now, $36^{\log_6 4} = (6^2)^{\log_6 4} = 6^{2 \log_6 4} = 6^{\log_6 (4^2)} = 6^{\log_6 16} = 16$.

questions.question_text: If $\log_{\sqrt{8}} x = 3\frac{1}{3}$, find the value of $x$.
Evaluate: (i) $\log_5 3 \times \log_{27} 25$ (ii) $\log_9 27 - \log_{27} 9$
questions.solution:
(i) $\log_5 3 \times \log_{27} 25 = \frac{\log 3}{\log 5} \times \frac{\log 25}{\log 27} = \frac{\log 3}{\log 5} \times \frac{\log 5^2}{\log 3^3} = \frac{\log 3}{\log 5} \times \frac{2 \log 5}{3 \log 3} = \frac{2}{3}$.
(ii) Let $\log_9 27 = n$. Then $9^n = 27 \Leftrightarrow 3^{2n} = 3^3 \Leftrightarrow 2n = 3 \Leftrightarrow n = \frac{3}{2}$.
Again, let $\log_{27} 9 = m$. Then $27^m = 9 \Leftrightarrow 3^{3m} = 3^2 \Leftrightarrow 3m = 2 \Leftrightarrow m = \frac{2}{3}$.
$\therefore \log_9 27 - \log_{27} 9 = (n - m) = \left(\frac{3}{2} - \frac{2}{3}\right) = \frac{5}{6}$.

questions.question_text: Simplify: $\log \frac{75}{16} - 2 \log \frac{5}{9} + \log \frac{32}{243}$
If $\log_2 [\log_3 (\log_2 x)] = 1$, find the value of $x$.
If $\log_{10}(x^2 - 6x + 45) = 2$, find the value of $x$.
questions.solution:
$\log_{10} (x^2 - 6x + 45) = 2 \Rightarrow x^2 - 6x + 45 = 10^2 = 100$
$\Rightarrow x^2 - 6x - 55 = 0 \Rightarrow x^2 - 11x + 5x - 55 = 0$
$\Rightarrow x (x - 11) + 5 (x - 11) = 0 \Rightarrow (x - 11) (x + 5) = 0$
$\Rightarrow x = 11$ or $x = -5$.

questions.question_text: Find the value of $x$ which satisfies the relation $\log_{10} 3 + \log_{10} (4x + 1) = \log_{10} (x + 1) + 1$
Simplify: $\frac{1}{\log_{xy} (xyz)} + \frac{1}{\log_{yz} (xyz)} + \frac{1}{\log_{zx} (xyz)}$
If $\log_a b = \frac{1}{2}, \log_b c = \frac{1}{3}$ and $\log_c a = \frac{k}{5}$, find the value of $k$.
questions.solution:
$\log_a b = \frac{1}{2}, \log_b c = \frac{1}{3}, \log_c a = \frac{k}{5} \Rightarrow \frac{\log b}{\log a} = \frac{1}{2}, \frac{\log c}{\log b} = \frac{1}{3}, \frac{\log a}{\log c} = \frac{k}{5}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{\log b}{\log a} \times \frac{\log c}{\log b} \times \frac{\log a}{\log c} = \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{k}{5} \Rightarrow 1 = \frac{k}{30} \Rightarrow k = 30$.

questions.question_text: If $\log_{10} 2 = 0.30103$, find the value of $\log_{10} 50$.

If $\log 2 = 0.30103$, find the number of digits in $2^{56}$.
questions.solution:
$\log (2^{56}) = 56 \log 2 = (56 \times 0.30103) = 16.85768$.
Its characteristic is 16. Hence, the number of digits in $2^{56}$ is 17.

If $\log_8 p = 25$ and $\log_2 q = 5$, then
questions.solution:
$\log_{8}p=25\text{ and }\log_{2}q=5\Rightarrow p=8^{25}\text{ and }q=2^{5}$
$\Rightarrow p=(2^{3})^{25}\text{ and }q=2^{5}$
$\Rightarrow p=2^{75}\text{ and }q=2^{5}$
$\Rightarrow p=(2^{5})^{15}\text{ and }q=2^{5}$
$\Rightarrow p=q^{15}.$

Which of the following statements is not correct ?
questions.solution:
(a) Since $\log_a a = 1$, so $\log_{10} 10 = 1$.
(b) $\log (2 + 3) = 5$ and $\log (2 \times 3) = \log 6 = \log 2 + \log 3$.
$\therefore \log (2 + 3) \neq \log (2 \times 3)$.
(c) Since $\log_a 1 = 0$, so $\log_{10} 1 = 0$.
(d) $\log (1 + 2 + 3) = \log 6 = \log (1 \times 2 \times 3) = \log 1 + \log 2 + \log 3$.
So, (b) is incorrect.

questions.solution:

$\Rightarrow x \log a = y \log b \Rightarrow \frac{x}{y} = \frac{\log b}{\log a}$.

If $\log_a (ab) = x$, then $\log_b (ab)$ is
questions.solution:
$\log_a (ab) = x \Leftrightarrow \frac{\log ab}{\log a} = x \Leftrightarrow \frac{\log a + \log b}{\log a} = x$
$\Leftrightarrow 1 + \frac{\log b}{\log a} = x \Leftrightarrow \frac{\log b}{\log a} = x - 1 \Leftrightarrow \frac{\log a}{\log b} = \frac{1}{x - 1}$
$\Leftrightarrow 1 + \frac{\log a}{\log b} = 1 + \frac{1}{x - 1} \Leftrightarrow \frac{\log b + \log a}{\log b} = \frac{x - 1 + 1}{x - 1}$
$\Leftrightarrow \frac{\log (ab)}{\log b} = \frac{x}{x - 1} \Leftrightarrow \log_b (ab) = \frac{x}{x - 1}$.

questions.question_text: If $\log_a m = x$, then $\log_{\frac{1}{a}} m$ equals
questions.solution: $\left(\frac{1}{m}\right)^{-1} = m^{-1} = \log_a m^{-1} = -1 \log_a m = -1 \times -1 \log_a m = \log_a m = x$.

questions.question_text: If $\log_{10} 2 = a$ and $\log_{10} 3 = b$, then $\log_5 12$ equals
If $\log 2 = x$, $\log 3 = y$ and $\log 7 = z$, then the value of $\log 3 (4 \cdot \sqrt{63})$ is
If $\log_4 x + \log_2 x = 6$, then $x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\log_4 x + \log_2 x = 6 \iff \frac{\log x}{\log 4} + \frac{\log x}{\log 2} = 6$
$\iff \frac{\log x}{2 \log 2} + \frac{\log x}{\log 2} = 6$
$\iff 3 \log x = 12 \log 2 \iff \log x = 4 \log 2$
$\iff \log x = \log (2^4) = \log 16 \iff x = 16$.

questions.question_text: If $\log_{10} (x^2 - 6x + 10) = 0$, then the value of $x$ is
If $\log_{10} x + \log_{10} y = 3$ and $\log_{10} x - \log_{10} y = 1$, then $x$ and $y$ are respectively
questions.solution:
$\log_{10} x + \log_{10} y = 3 \dots(i)$
$\log_{10} x - \log_{10} y = 1 \dots(ii)$
Adding (i) and (ii), we get:
$2 \log_{10} x = 4$ or $\log_{10} x = 2 \implies x = 10^2 = 100$.
Also, $\log_{10} y = 3 - \log_{10} x = 3 - 2 = 1 \implies y = 10^1 = 10$.
Hence, $x = 100, y = 10$.

$(\log_5 5) (\log_4 9) (\log_3 2)$ is equal to
If $\log_{12} 27 = a$, then $\log_6 16$ is
questions.solution:
$\log_{12} 27 = a \implies \frac{\log 27}{\log 12} = a \implies \frac{3 \log 3}{\log (3 \times 2^2)} = a$
$\implies \frac{3 \log 3}{\log 3 + 2 \log 2} = a \implies 3 \log 3 = a \log 3 + 2a \log 2$
$\implies \log 3 (3 - a) = 2a \log 2 \implies \log 3 = \frac{2a}{3 - a} \log 2$.
$\log_6 16 = \frac{\log 16}{\log 6} = \frac{4 \log 2}{\log 2 + \log 3} = \frac{4 \log 2}{\log 2 + \frac{2a}{3 - a} \log 2}$
$= \frac{4}{\frac{3 - a + 2a}{3 - a}} = \frac{4(3 - a)}{3 + a}$.

questions.question_text: If $\log_{10} 5 + \log_{10} (5x + 1) = \log_{10} (x + 5) + 1$, then $x$ is equal to
If $\log_5 (x^2 + x) - \log_5 (x + 1) = 2$, then the value of $x$ is
$\frac{1}{2} (\log x + \log y)$ will equal $\log \left(\frac{x+y}{2}\right)$ if
questions.solution:
$\frac{1}{2} (\log x + \log y) = \log \left(\frac{x + y}{2}\right) \implies \log (xy) = \log \left(\frac{x + y}{2}\right)^2$
$\implies xy = \left(\frac{x + y}{2}\right)^2$
$\implies 4xy = x^2 + y^2 + 2xy$
$\implies x^2 + y^2 - 2xy = 0$
$\implies (x - y)^2 = 0 \implies x - y = 0 \implies x = y$.

If $\log x + \log y = \log (x + y)$, then
questions.solution:
$\log x + \log y = \log (x + y)$
$\implies \log (xy) = \log (x + y)$
$\implies x + y = xy \implies y (x - 1) = x \implies y = \frac{x}{x - 1}$.

questions.question_text: If $\log \frac{a}{b} + \log \frac{b}{a} = \log (a + b)$, then
$\left[\log \left(\frac{a^2}{bc}\right) + \log \left(\frac{b^2}{ac}\right) + \log \left(\frac{c^2}{ab}\right)\right]$ is equal to
$\frac{1}{\log_a b} \times \frac{1}{\log_b c} \times \frac{1}{\log_c a}$ is equal to
questions.solution: Given expression = $\left( \frac{\log a}{\log b} \times \frac{\log b}{\log c} \times \frac{\log c}{\log a} \right) = 1$.

If $a = b^x$, $b = c^y$ and $c = a^z$, then the value of $xyz$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$a = b^x, b = c^y, c = a^z \Rightarrow x = \log_{b} a, y = \log_{c} b, z = \log_{a} c$
$\Rightarrow xyz = (\log_{b} a) \times (\log_{c} b) \times (\log_{a} c)$
$\Rightarrow xyz = \left( \frac{\log a}{\log b} \right) \times \left( \frac{\log b}{\log c} \right) \times \left( \frac{\log c}{\log a} \right) = 1$.

If $\log_3 x + \log_9 x^2 + \log_{27} x^3 = 9$, then $x$ equals
questions.solution:
$\log_{3} x + \log_{a} x^2 + \log_{27} x^3 = 9 \Rightarrow \log_{3} x + \log_{3^2} x^2 + \log_{3^3} x^3 = 9$
$\Rightarrow \log_{3} x + \frac{2}{2} \log_{3} x + \frac{3}{3} \log_{3} x = 9 \Rightarrow 3 \log_{3} x = 9 \Rightarrow \log_{3} x = 3 \Rightarrow x = 3^3 = 27$.

If $\log 2 = 0.30103$, the number of digits in $2^{64}$ is
questions.solution:
$\log (2^{64}) = 64 \times \log 2 = (64 \times 0.30103) = 19.26592.$
Its characteristic is $19.$ Hence, the number of digits in $2^{64}$
is $20.$

questions.question_text: If $\log 2 = 0.30103$, the number of digits in $4^{50}$ is
questions.solution:
$\log 4^{50} = 50 \log 4 = 50 \log 2^2 = (50 \times 2) \log 2 = 100 \times \log 2$
$= (100 \times 0.30103) = 30.103.$
$\therefore$ Characteristic = $30.$ Hence, the number of digits in
$4^{50} = 31.$

questions.question_text: If $\log 2 = 0.30103$, then the number of digits in $5^{20}$ is
questions.solution:
$\log 5^{20} = 20 \log 5 = 20 \left[\log \left(\frac{10}{2}\right)\right] = 20 (\log 10 - \log 2)$
$= 20 (1 - 0.3010) = 20 \times 0.6990 = 13.9800.$
$\therefore$ Characteristic = $13.$ Hence, the number of digits in
$5^{20}$ is $14.$

questions.question_text: If $\log 2 = 0.30103$, $\log 3 = 0.47712$, then the number of digits in $6^{20}$ is
questions.solution:
$\log 6^{20} = 20 \log (2 \times 3) = 20 (\log 2 + \log 3)$
$= 20 (0.30103 + 0.47712)$
$= 20 \times 0.77815 = 15.563.$
$\therefore$ Characteristic = $15.$ Hence, the number of digits in
$6^{20} = 16.$

questions.question_text: The number of digits in $4^9 \times 5^{17}$, when expressed in usual form, is
If $\log 3$, $\log (3^x - 2)$ and $\log (3^x + 4)$ are in arithmetic progression, then $x$ is equal to
questions.solution:
In arithmetic progression common ratios are equal to
$\log (3^x - 2) - \log 3 = \log (3^x + 4) - \log (3^x - 2)$
$\frac{\log (3^x - 2)}{\log 3} = \frac{\log (3^x + 4)}{\log (3^x - 2)}$
$\log 3^x \log 3^x = \log 4 \log 2$
$\log 2 \log 3 = \log 3$
$x = \log_4 \log_2 \log_2$
$x = \log 8$
$x = \log 2^3$
